title: Tax Optimization Quiz - Are You Leaving Money on the Table? | CrunchMilk
metaDescription: Discover how well you're optimizing your taxes with this free 8-question quiz. Learn if you're missing deductions, credits, and tax-saving strategies.
tagline: Are you paying more in taxes than you need to?

questions.question: How do you currently file your taxes?

options.text: I don't file or I'm behind on taxes
I use free software and take the standard deduction
I use tax software and review itemized vs. standard
I work with a CPA or tax professional who does proactive planning

questions.question: Are you maximizing tax-advantaged retirement accounts (401k, IRA, HSA)?

options.text: I don't contribute to any of these
I contribute some but far from the limits
I max out one or two accounts
I max out all available tax-advantaged accounts

questions.question: Do you have any side income, freelance work, or small business income?

options.text: Yes, and I don't track expenses for it
Yes, I track income but not deductible expenses
Yes, I track both income and major deductions
Yes, with full expense tracking, estimated quarterly payments, and business structure optimization

questions.question: Do you know if you qualify for tax credits like the Child Tax Credit, Earned Income Credit, or education credits?

options.text: I don't know what credits I might qualify for
I claim one or two obvious ones
I review available credits each year
I strategically plan to maximize every credit I qualify for

questions.question: How do you handle investment gains and losses for tax purposes?

options.text: I don't consider taxes when investing
I'm aware of capital gains but don't plan around them
I do some tax-loss harvesting at year end
I strategically harvest losses, manage holding periods, and place assets in tax-efficient accounts

questions.question: Are you making charitable contributions in a tax-efficient way?

options.text: I don't donate or don't track donations
I donate and keep receipts for deductions
I bundle donations or use donor-advised funds
I donate appreciated assets, use QCDs, or bunch deductions strategically

questions.question: Do you adjust your W-4 withholding to match your actual tax liability?

options.text: I haven't updated my W-4 in years
I set it when I started my job and forgot about it
I review it occasionally after life changes
I optimize it annually to avoid large refunds or underpayment

questions.question: Do you do any year-end tax planning before December 31st?

options.text: No, I wait until filing season to think about taxes
I sometimes rush contributions in December
I review my situation and make some moves in Q4
I do comprehensive year-end planning including Roth conversions, income timing, and deduction acceleration

scoreRanges.label: Leaving Money Behind
scoreRanges.analysis: You're likely overpaying on taxes significantly. Start with the basics: make sure you're contributing to tax-advantaged accounts and tracking all possible deductions. Even small changes could save you thousands.

scoreRanges.label: Room to Improve
scoreRanges.analysis: You handle the basics but are missing intermediate strategies. Consider reviewing your withholding, exploring tax credits you might qualify for, and being more intentional about investment tax efficiency.

scoreRanges.label: Well Optimized
scoreRanges.analysis: You're doing a good job minimizing your tax burden. Look into advanced strategies like Roth conversion ladders, asset location optimization, and strategic charitable giving to squeeze out more savings.

scoreRanges.label: Tax Pro
scoreRanges.analysis: You're maximizing nearly every tax advantage available. You likely save thousands annually through strategic planning. Keep staying current with tax law changes and continue your proactive approach.
